LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY AND ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE FACT THAT CERTAIN DEFENDANTS HAVE BEEN DISMISSED DOES NOT, WITHOUT MORE, RENDER THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING THOSE DEFENDANTS SUBJECT TO A MOTION TO STRIKE, PARTICULARLY WHERE, AS HERE, PLAINTIFF IS PURSUING A THEORY OF CONSPIRACY INVOLVING THOSE DEFENDANTS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO ATTEND AN IME, THE IME WILL REQUIRE PLAINTIFF TO TRAVEL APPROXIMATELY 82 MILES EACH WAY. AT PLAINTIFF'S OPTION, HE MAY CHOOSE TO DIVIDE THE TRAVEL OVER THREE DAYS SO THAT HE IS NOT REQUIRED TO TRAVEL MORE THAN 50 MILES PER DAY AND THE DEFENDANT MUST PAY THE COST OF A LIMOUSINE DRIVER TO TRANSPORT PLAINTIFF BY LIMOUSINE TO THE IME AS WELL AS REASONABLE COSTS OF MEALS AND LODGING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT, JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077. =302/HEK

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical Exmination Of Pltf
Off Calendar Nov-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LAWRENCE A. MANN OF BOURHIS & MANN, TEL. NO. 392-4660 EXT. 112 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. JOHN T. BURNITE OF WILSON ELSER, TEL. NO. 625-9255 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Exmination Of Plaintiff IS Granted in part. Plaintiff is required to attend an IME, The IME will require plaintiff to travel approximately 82 miles each way. At plaintiff's option, he may choose to divide the travel over three days so that he is not required to travel more than 50 miles per day and the defendant must pay the cost of a limousine driver to transport plaintiff by limousine to the IME as well as reasonable costs of meals and lodging. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT, Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077. =302/HEK

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT THE COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DUPLICATE ENTRY. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KAREN W. YIU, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5385 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend, if plaintiff is able to do so, to allege that the Insurance Commissioner breached a specific mandatory duty, which the complaint does not now do. (Schwartz v. Poizner (2010) 187 Cal. App. 4th 572). A writ of mandate cannot be based on general enforcement provisions or statutes involving the defendant's exercise of discretion. Plaintiff also fails to allege facts showing that the defendant abused his discretion by acting arbitrarily. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR # 11604 =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2011 Continued To Oct-27-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT THE COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant Commissioner of the Department of Insurance's Demurrer to Complaint is continued to October 27, 2011 per amended notice. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS REASSURE AMERICA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY AND ROYAL MACCABEES LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' motion to strike first amended complaint is denied. The fact that certain defendants have been dismissed does not, without more, render the allegations regarding those defendants subject to a motion to strike, particularly where, as here, plaintiff is pursuing a theory of conspiracy involving those defendants. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
